header.strapline: China-side sourcing, export, relocation, and hiring support from Zhuhai.

footer.title: Clear execution for cross-border work in China.
footer.description: Pomerol International Trading (Zhuhai) Co., Ltd helps overseas teams run sourcing, export, relocation, and recruitment work with tighter scope and clearer milestones.
footer.responseTarget: Typical first reply target: within 1 business day.
footer.legalNote: All work is delivered against written scope, milestone control, and final contract terms.

metadata.title: China Sourcing, Export, and Operations Support
metadata.description: Pomerol International helps overseas teams with China sourcing, export execution, relocation support, and recruitment coordination.

hero.title: China Sourcing, Export, and Operations Support
hero.description: A concise China-side operating partner for sourcing, export execution, relocation support, and hiring coordination.

serviceSection.title: Three clear ways to start
serviceSection.summary: The homepage stays short by grouping the work into product, sourcing, and people-related tracks.

serviceGroups.title: Product Supply
serviceGroups.summary: 3C export and industrial hardware programs with supplier coordination, QC, and export file control.

serviceGroups.title: Supplier Sourcing
serviceGroups.summary: China-side supplier search, quote alignment, and pilot-order follow-up for procurement teams.

serviceGroups.title: Relocation & Hiring
serviceGroups.summary: Visa-relocation case support and recruitment coordination for companies operating in China.

bestFit.title: One China-side partner, fewer moving parts.
bestFit.description: Send a short brief. The first reply focuses on scope, feasibility, and the next step.
bullets: Import, sourcing, relocation, and hiring use cases

processSection.title: How engagement starts
processSection.summary: Define the brief, test feasibility, then execute against milestones.

processSteps.title: Brief and scope
processSteps.detail: We confirm the service line, target market or city, timeline, and constraints before any commitment.

processSteps.title: Feasibility and risk check
processSteps.detail: We test supplier fit, document complexity, delivery assumptions, or case readiness before moving forward.

processSteps.title: Milestone execution
processSteps.detail: Execution follows a written plan with progress updates, issue handling, and final handover.

operatingPrinciples.title: Clear scope first
operatingPrinciples.summary: The first response defines what is in scope instead of overselling uncertain outcomes.

operatingPrinciples.title: Milestone visibility
operatingPrinciples.summary: Clients get a simple path for progress updates, decisions, and escalation.

operatingPrinciples.title: Documented closure
operatingPrinciples.summary: Every project closes with files, notes, and open-item status.

riskSection.title: Risk control and boundaries
riskSection.summary: We focus on the issues that most often derail cross-border work.

riskItems.title: Supplier capability mismatch
riskItems.signal: A factory looks acceptable on paper but cannot support the real delivery baseline.
riskItems.control: Capability, assumptions, and constraints are checked before final alignment.

riskItems.title: Document gaps
riskItems.signal: Invoice, packing, specification, or submission files do not match the actual requirement.
riskItems.control: Critical files are checked against the agreed baseline before shipment or submission.

riskItems.title: Timeline drift
riskItems.signal: Approval loops, production changes, or missing inputs start pushing the original schedule.
riskItems.control: Milestone updates and early warning surface issues before they become late surprises.

boundaryPoints: Scope is confirmed in writing before execution starts.
Government approvals, customs outcomes, and third-party certifications stay outside provider control.
Pricing and lead time depend on declared quantity, baseline, and commercial assumptions.

faq.question: What is the fastest way to start?
faq.answer: Send the service line, target market or city, timeline, and key constraints by email.

faq.question: Can you support both products and people-related cases?
faq.answer: Yes. The site covers product supply, sourcing, relocation support, and recruitment coordination.

faq.question: Do you guarantee regulatory or customs outcomes?
faq.answer: No. We support preparation and process control, but authority decisions remain outside our control.

metadata.title: About Pomerol International
metadata.description: Company profile, operating model, and execution controls of Pomerol International Trading (Zhuhai) Co., Ltd.

hero.title: About Pomerol International
hero.description: A Zhuhai-based service company supporting overseas clients in product supply, sourcing, relocation process guidance, and recruitment coordination in China.

facts.label: Service model
facts.value: B2B projects and recurring execution support

coverageSection.title: What the company handles
coverageSection.summary: The business runs across five operating lines with practical delivery responsibility.

coverageItems: 3C electronics export execution for international buyers.
Industrial computer and embedded hardware sourcing support.
Supplier verification and procurement coordination in China.
Visa and relocation process support for foreign professionals.
Recruitment and job-agent support for hiring in China.

processSection.title: How projects are executed
processSection.summary: Delivery follows a practical control model to keep commercial expectations aligned with real execution.

processSteps.title: Scope confirmation
processSteps.detail: Each assignment is mapped to deliverables, owners, and checkpoints before engagement starts.

processSteps.title: Cross-function execution
processSteps.detail: Procurement, QC, logistics, and case coordinators work against one shared milestone plan.

processSteps.title: Documented closure
processSteps.detail: Completion is recorded through delivery files and written closure notes for traceability.

controlSection.title: Control framework
controlSection.summary: The framework is designed to reduce ambiguity, rework, and communication drift.

controlItems.title: Scope governance
controlItems.signal: Open-ended requests increase uncertainty and timeline risk.
controlItems.control: Written scope with controlled change handling.

controlItems.title: Communication discipline
controlItems.signal: Multiple informal channels create conflicting instructions.

controlItems.title: Documentation integrity
controlItems.signal: Different versions of the same file circulate across teams.
controlItems.control: One reference version with dated revisions.

controlItems.title: Commercial transparency
controlItems.signal: Assumptions are missing from quote notes.
controlItems.control: Pricing and execution assumptions stated in every proposal.

boundarySection.title: Operational boundaries
boundarySection.summary: The company provides process execution support, not open-ended guarantees.

boundaryPoints: Regulatory approvals and decisions by public authorities are outside provider control.
Outcome expectations depend on declared requirements and client-provided data.
Third-party performance is managed through controls but not guaranteed.
Commercial commitments are valid only when stated in signed scope documents.

metadata.title: Product Supply Programs from China
metadata.description: 3C export and industrial computer supply programs from China with QC checkpoints and export document control.

hero.title: Product supply programs
hero.description: The product line covers 3C electronics export and industrial computers with written specification baselines, supplier verification, and delivery control.

tracksSection.title: Choose the relevant track
tracksSection.summary: Pick the path based on category and technical complexity.

tracks.title: 3C Electronics Export
tracks.description: Consumer electronics accessories and selected device categories for overseas wholesale programs.

tracks.title: Industrial Computers & Embedded Systems
tracks.description: Fanless IPC, embedded boards, and related accessories for engineering and distribution channels.

workflowSection.title: Shared delivery workflow
workflowSection.summary: The same control structure is used across both product tracks to reduce variance.

workflowSteps.title: Specification and commercial alignment
workflowSteps.detail: Baseline, acceptance criteria, target market, and packaging needs are checked before quotation.

workflowSteps.title: Supplier quotation and capacity screening
workflowSteps.detail: We screen manufacturing stability, lead-time reliability, and export document readiness.

workflowSteps.title: Pre-shipment control and final confirmation
workflowSteps.detail: Sampling, labeling checks, and shipping mark confirmation are completed before dispatch.

documentSection.title: Standard document package
documentSection.summary: Items vary by country or contract, but this set is the normal baseline.

rows: Declares transaction value and cargo description
Confirms quantity, weight, and package structure
Records technical baseline and revision reference
Captures sampling checks and acceptance status
Aligns consignee data, marks, and route details

riskSection.title: Risk control and boundaries
riskSection.summary: Execution quality depends on complete technical input and timely client confirmation.

riskItems.title: Incorrect product declaration
riskItems.signal: Shipping descriptions do not match the PO or specification sheet.
riskItems.control: Final document reconciliation before shipment booking.

riskItems.title: Lead-time slippage
riskItems.signal: Factory planning conflicts or missing critical components appear near production start.
riskItems.control: Milestone updates with early warning and alternate supplier paths when needed.

riskItems.title: Batch inconsistency
riskItems.signal: Packaging, firmware, or hardware revision changes across lots.
riskItems.control: Batch-level checklists and reference sample signoff before mass release.

riskItems.title: Unclear after-sales expectations
riskItems.signal: No agreed process for defect reporting or replacement handling.
riskItems.control: Written after-sales scope and response timeline in the contract annex.

boundaryPoints: Minimum order quantity and lead time depend on supplier and category.
Special certification requirements must be declared before quotation.
Freight, insurance, and customs terms are finalized in contract by incoterm.
Warranty handling follows the agreed service appendix and is not open-ended.

metadata.title: Contact and Inquiry Intake
metadata.description: Structured inquiry intake for sourcing, export, industrial hardware, relocation, and recruitment services.

hero.title: Contact and inquiry intake
hero.description: Please send a structured inquiry so we can assess feasibility, risks, and likely process steps before commitment.

directSection.title: Direct contact
directSection.summary: Initial communication is handled by phone or email.

directCards.title: Phone
directCards.description: Best for urgent clarification on active projects.

directCards.title: Email
directCards.description: Best for document-based inquiries and formal requirement submission.

inquirySection.title: Recommended inquiry format
inquirySection.summary: Providing these fields in the first message shortens qualification time.

rows: Routes the inquiry to the right team
Expected volume and required completion date
Product specs or candidate profile details

controlSection.title: Communication controls and boundaries
controlSection.summary: These controls keep intake precise and execution aligned.

controlItems.title: Incomplete project brief
controlItems.signal: The inquiry omits quantity, destination market, or timeline.
controlItems.control: Use the intake checklist to provide a usable baseline.

controlItems.title: Scope ambiguity at kickoff
controlItems.signal: Requested output spans multiple service lines without priorities.
controlItems.control: Break the inquiry into modules and confirm one written scope summary.

controlItems.title: Delayed decision loops
controlItems.signal: Extended response gaps create timing risk for production, hiring, or submission.
controlItems.control: Nominate a primary contact and agree a response cadence.

controlItems.title: Version conflict in documents
controlItems.signal: Multiple files with overlapping names arrive without clear status.
controlItems.control: Issue a dated master document list and mark replaced versions clearly.

boundaryPoints: Formal commitment starts only after written scope and commercial terms are confirmed.
Response windows vary by inquiry complexity and file completeness.
Confidential files should be shared only through agreed channels with named recipients.
Out-of-scope requests can be assessed separately as added modules.

metadata.title: China Supplier Sourcing Service
metadata.description: Supplier sourcing workflow in China covering verification, quotation alignment, pilot monitoring, and risk control.

hero.title: China supplier sourcing service
hero.description: Structured supplier discovery and validation for overseas buyers who need cleaner comparison and tighter pilot execution.

facts.label: Outputs
facts.value: Supplier shortlist, normalized quotes, pilot report

firstSection.title: Service modules
firstSection.summary: Modules can be used as a full cycle or selected to match procurement maturity.

rows: Market scan and longlist aligned to the product brief
Initial supplier map with profile notes
Pilot-order tracking and issue escalation support

processSection.title: Supplier verification workflow
processSection.summary: Verification is handled in stages with objective evidence at each gate.

processSteps.title: Requirement briefing and feasibility screen
processSteps.detail: Product target, volume estimate, quality standard, and commercial limits are checked first.

processSteps.title: Supplier longlist and profile validation
processSteps.detail: Candidate suppliers are screened for registration, manufacturing fit, and delivery relevance.

processSteps.title: Quotation normalization and sample coordination
processSteps.detail: Quotes are normalized for direct comparison and samples are coordinated for validation.

processSteps.title: Pilot monitoring and handover
processSteps.detail: Pilot progress, exceptions, and output quality are tracked with a written handover report.

thirdSection.title: Risk controls
thirdSection.summary: These controls reduce false positives in supplier selection and pilot execution.

thirdRiskItems.title: Supplier profile inconsistency
thirdRiskItems.signal: Claimed capability does not match the observed production setup.
thirdRiskItems.control: Checklist-based verification and evidence scoring before recommendation.

thirdRiskItems.title: Quotation ambiguity
thirdRiskItems.signal: Tooling, packaging, or after-sales assumptions are hidden in the quote.
thirdRiskItems.control: Line-by-line quote normalization with assumption disclosure.

thirdRiskItems.title: Sample-to-mass gap
thirdRiskItems.signal: Mass production diverges from the approved sample benchmark.

thirdRiskItems.title: Unclear ownership
thirdRiskItems.signal: Buyer, supplier, and intermediary actions are not time-bound or traceable.
thirdRiskItems.control: Written ownership matrix and milestone log.

fourthSection.title: Operational boundaries
fourthSection.summary: The following limits are defined before engagement.

fourthList: We provide process support and analysis, not a legal guarantee of future supplier performance.
Laboratory certification testing is arranged through qualified third parties when required.
Final purchase contracts are signed directly between buyer and supplier unless agreed otherwise.
Cost savings are not promised without fixed demand volume and stable specification.

metadata.title: Visa and Relocation Support in China
metadata.description: Visa and relocation support for foreign professionals and employers, including case pre-check, document planning, and process follow-up.

hero.title: Visa and relocation consulting
hero.description: Structured case preparation and relocation support for foreign professionals and hiring companies operating in China.

facts.label: Main users
facts.value: Foreign professionals and employer HR teams

facts.label: Boundary
facts.value: Decision authority remains with government departments

firstSection.title: Service scope
firstSection.summary: The service focuses on operational preparation and guidance for compliant case handling.

firstList: Initial route review based on applicant profile and city-level practice.
Document checklist preparation for the applicant and sponsoring employer.
Submission sequence planning and status follow-up support.
Practical relocation guidance for agreed post-arrival tasks.

processSection.title: Case workflow
processSection.summary: Each case follows staged controls to reduce rejections and timeline misunderstandings.

processSteps.title: Eligibility pre-check
processSteps.detail: Applicant profile, job-offer status, and destination-city requirements are reviewed first.

processSteps.title: Document planning and checklist issue
processSteps.detail: A case-specific checklist is prepared, including translation or legalization requirements where needed.

processSteps.title: Submission coordination and follow-up
processSteps.detail: Submission sequencing is tracked with reminders and correction loops.

processSteps.title: Arrival and relocation handover
processSteps.detail: After approval, we assist with practical onboarding steps according to local process and agreed scope.

thirdSection.title: Risk disclosure and controls
thirdSection.summary: The following signals are actively managed during case execution.

thirdRiskItems.title: Incomplete applicant file
thirdRiskItems.signal: Missing notarization, expired certificates, or inconsistent background information.
thirdRiskItems.control: Pre-submission document audit and correction cycle.

thirdRiskItems.title: Policy interpretation mismatch
thirdRiskItems.signal: Assumptions are based on another city or an outdated policy version.
thirdRiskItems.control: Case advice is tied to current local guidance at the time of handling.

thirdRiskItems.title: Unrealistic timeline expectation
thirdRiskItems.signal: Travel or employment plans are fixed before approval milestones are secured.
thirdRiskItems.control: Timeline is communicated as a range with dependency notes.

thirdRiskItems.title: Scope misunderstanding after arrival
thirdRiskItems.signal: Requests extend beyond the agreed relocation tasks.
thirdRiskItems.control: Written checklist with out-of-scope items identified early.

fourthSection.title: Service limits
fourthSection.summary: These boundaries protect practical expectations in every case.

fourthList: Visa approval outcomes and processing speed are controlled by relevant authorities.
Policy updates may affect active cases; checklist changes are communicated in writing.
Legal representation in disputes is outside this consulting scope unless separately engaged.
Travel bookings should be finalized only after key approvals are secured.

metadata.title: Recruitment and Hiring Support in China
metadata.description: Recruitment support for hiring in China, including role intake, screening coordination, interview flow, and onboarding handover.

hero.title: Recruitment and job-agent service
hero.description: Structured hiring support for employers and foreign professionals with emphasis on role clarity and communication discipline.

firstSection.title: Coverage scope
firstSection.summary: Scope is defined by role complexity, hiring urgency, and required market reach.

rows: Requirement intake and targeted candidate pipeline
Coordination between recruitment and visa support tasks
Compressed search cycle with weekly progress updates

processSection.title: Recruitment workflow
processSection.summary: The workflow keeps hiring teams and candidates aligned on timeline, criteria, and ownership.

processSteps.title: Role intake and criteria lock
processSteps.detail: Position requirements, compensation range, reporting line, and key thresholds are documented first.

processSteps.title: Candidate sourcing and screening
processSteps.detail: Candidate pipelines are built through targeted channels and screened against agreed criteria.

processSteps.title: Interview coordination and feedback loop
processSteps.detail: Scheduling, structured feedback capture, and comparison notes keep decision cycles clear.

processSteps.title: Offer process and onboarding handover
processSteps.detail: Offer terms are coordinated with a written record of acceptance conditions and onboarding timing.

thirdSection.title: Quality and confidentiality controls
thirdSection.summary: Controls focus on reducing rework, candidate drop-off, and communication confusion.

thirdRiskItems.title: Role definition drift
thirdRiskItems.signal: Hiring criteria change repeatedly after the search has started.
thirdRiskItems.control: Formal role-freeze point with change-note protocol.

thirdRiskItems.title: Candidate expectation mismatch
thirdRiskItems.signal: Compensation, location, or visa conditions diverge between briefing and offer stage.
thirdRiskItems.control: Expectation checks at screening and pre-offer stages.

thirdRiskItems.title: Confidentiality leakage
thirdRiskItems.signal: Sensitive hiring information is shared beyond authorized participants.

thirdRiskItems.title: Slow decision cycles
thirdRiskItems.signal: Interview feedback delays lead to candidate loss.
thirdRiskItems.control: Agreed decision timeline with escalation points.

fourthSection.title: Operational boundaries
fourthSection.summary: This service supports process execution and does not replace employer responsibilities.

fourthList: Final hiring decisions, contract terms, and compliance duties remain with the employer.
Candidate acceptance cannot be guaranteed because market conditions and timing vary.
Background checks and legal screening are performed by authorized third parties when required.
Retention outcomes depend on post-hire management and sit outside recruitment process control.

metadata.title: 3C Electronics Export from China
metadata.description: Category scope, execution workflow, and risk controls for China-based 3C electronics export projects.

hero.title: 3C electronics export
hero.description: Controlled sourcing and export execution for overseas buyers of 3C electronics and related accessories.

facts.label: Output
facts.value: Shipment-ready goods and final document set

firstSection.title: Category scope and typical MOQ
firstSection.summary: MOQ and lead time vary by item and manufacturing route.

processSection.title: Execution process
processSection.summary: Each project moves through fixed checkpoints to align technical baseline and shipment readiness.

processSteps.title: Category and compliance intake
processSteps.detail: Target markets, packaging language, and labeling references are confirmed before supplier matching.

processSteps.title: Supplier quote and sample verification
processSteps.detail: Quoted models are screened for production consistency and samples are checked against baseline requirements.

processSteps.title: Production planning and QC checkpoints
processSteps.detail: Key dates are fixed for material readiness, pilot-lot validation, and pre-shipment review.

processSteps.title: Shipment release and handover
processSteps.detail: Commercial files, packing data, and shipping instruction alignment are finalized before release.

thirdSection.title: Documentation and compliance handling
thirdSection.summary: Export file quality is treated as part of delivery quality.

thirdList: Commercial invoice and packing list are verified against PO line items and approved model identifiers.
Shipping marks, carton labels, and pallet references are cross-checked before loading release.
Customer-specific declaration formats are incorporated when provided before quote freeze.
Inspection records and shipment photos can be added to the closure package on request.

fourthSection.title: Risk signals and service limits
fourthSection.summary: These controls reduce disputes and late-stage failures in cross-border 3C orders.

fourthRiskItems.title: Model substitution during production
fourthRiskItems.signal: A supplier proposes component replacement after PO confirmation.
fourthRiskItems.control: No substitution without a written impact statement and customer approval.

fourthRiskItems.title: Labeling non-conformance
fourthRiskItems.signal: Packaging text, barcode format, or safety labels do not match market requirements.
fourthRiskItems.control: Packaging artwork and carton marks are signed off before mass production.

fourthRiskItems.title: Unstable batch quality
fourthRiskItems.signal: Defect rates rise between sample batch and shipment batch.
fourthRiskItems.control: Batch sampling protocol and shipment hold option until corrective action is complete.

fourthRiskItems.title: Unexpected port-side delay
fourthRiskItems.signal: Late document correction requests appear near booking cutoff.
fourthRiskItems.control: Document freeze deadline and final pre-booking audit.

fourthList: Retail-scale single-piece orders are outside scope.
Certification issuance remains with authorized bodies and cannot be guaranteed.
Transit insurance terms are defined in contract and are not implied by default.
Post-arrival local compliance obligations remain under importer responsibility.

metadata.title: Industrial Computers and Embedded Systems
metadata.description: Technical supply workflow and delivery controls for industrial computers and embedded hardware sourced from China.

hero.title: Industrial computers and embedded systems
hero.description: Stable China-side supply support for technical buyers who need baseline control, revision traceability, and deployment-fit documentation.

facts.label: Typical clients
facts.value: System integrators, OEM teams, technical distributors

facts.label: Common products
facts.value: Fanless IPC, panel PC, embedded boards, edge gateways

firstSection.title: Supply scope
firstSection.summary: These categories are commonly supported. Final availability depends on project requirements.

rows: Intel or ARM platforms with multi-I/O options

processSection.title: Engineering delivery workflow
processSection.summary: Technical projects follow a defined path to avoid post-order specification conflict.

processSteps.title: Application profile and baseline definition
processSteps.detail: The client provides deployment scenario, performance needs, interface limits, and environment conditions.

processSteps.title: Candidate matching and lifecycle review
processSteps.detail: Shortlisted hardware is reviewed for I/O fit, expansion needs, and lifecycle stability.

processSteps.title: Sample validation and revision lock
processSteps.detail: Pilot units are tested and revision code plus firmware reference are frozen before batch order.

processSteps.title: Order execution and handover
processSteps.detail: Production progress, packaging specification, and shipping files are tracked through closure.

thirdSection.title: Technical file control
thirdSection.summary: Project closure includes references that support batch consistency and field deployment.

thirdList: Baseline specification sheet with interface, voltage, and expansion notes.
Revision reference for hardware and firmware when supplied by the manufacturer.
Packing and labeling file aligned with site logistics requirements.
Issue log for approved deviations from the original baseline.

fourthSection.title: Reliability controls and project limits
fourthSection.summary: Controls focus on lifecycle, compatibility, and documented support boundaries.

fourthRiskItems.title: Lifecycle mismatch
fourthRiskItems.signal: The selected platform enters a discontinuation risk window too early.
fourthRiskItems.control: Lifecycle statement review and optional alternate BOM planning.

fourthRiskItems.title: Interface incompatibility
fourthRiskItems.signal: Site peripherals require connectors or protocols outside the baseline.
fourthRiskItems.control: Interface mapping review before sample release.

fourthRiskItems.title: Thermal or environmental failure
fourthRiskItems.signal: Operating profile exceeds tested temperature or humidity range.
fourthRiskItems.control: Early environment declaration and model selection against rated range.

fourthRiskItems.title: Inadequate support expectation
fourthRiskItems.signal: The project assumes overseas onsite support that is not contractually defined.
fourthRiskItems.control: Written support matrix listing remote and onsite responsibilities.

fourthList: Custom industrial design and certification engineering are not included unless separately scoped.
Long-term spare-stock strategy requires explicit forecasting by the buyer.
Site commissioning outside China is coordinated only if contract terms include it.
Performance under non-declared extreme conditions is outside warranty commitment.
